Title: 'Contemporary films about the Occupation in France are sounding alarm bells, with varying degrees of subtlety'
Description: The abundance of films about World War II helps explain how the gradual abandonment of democratic and republican values led to disaster, writes historian Henry Rousso in this op-ed.

Title: 'Sometimes I wake up in the night and I can't move': What is sleep paralysis?
Description: This temporary phenomenon is linked to the persistence of muscle atonia associated with REM sleep. It can come with visual, auditory, or tactile hallucinations, often frightening.

Title: The troubling Russian connections of a 'European' password manager
Description: Although it presents itself as a Spanish company, Passwork was actually developed in Russia and operates with a license from Russia's FSB domestic intelligence agency. The situation raises major security questions for its customers, including several French companies.

'It's Mad Max on the ocean': The cocaine-loaded speedboats racing to Europe's shores
Description: In the Atlantic, hundreds of powerful go-fast boats, custom-built to transport tons of cocaine, retrieve shipments from vessels traveling commercial routes between South America, Africa and Europe, according to an investigation by Le Monde in partnership with NDR, WDR, Süddeutsche Zeitung, NRC, IrpiMedia and The Washington Post.
Byline: Liselotte Mas,  Elsa Delmas and  Thomas Saintourens

Title: How the 2016 Nice terrorist attack unfolded, minute-by-minute
Description: On July 14, 2016, a terrorist drove a truck into crowds gathered on the famed Promenade des Anglais in the southern French city, killing 86 people. Ten years later, Le Monde looks back at how the tragedy unfolded.
